Bluetooth compliance test functions for starting, stopping, and entering HCI passthrough mode.

# Purpose
This C header file declares a set of functions related to Bluetooth testing operations. The functions [`bt_test_start`](<#bt_test_start>) and [`bt_test_stop`](<#bt_test_stop>) are used to initiate and terminate Bluetooth tests, respectively. The function [`bt_test_bt_sig_rf_test_mode`](<#bt_test_bt_sig_rf_test_mode>) checks if the Bluetooth device is in RF test mode, returning a boolean value. The function [`bt_test_enter_hci_passthrough`](<#bt_test_enter_hci_passthrough>) enables HCI passthrough mode, allowing direct communication with the Bluetooth hardware. The file includes standard headers `stdbool.h` and `stdint.h` to use boolean and integer types.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`
- `stdint.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### bt\_test\_start<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_test_start}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/bt_compliance_tests.h#L22>)

Starts the Bluetooth test mode.
- **Description**: Use this function to enable Bluetooth test mode. Before calling, ensure that test mode is not already active by stopping it with `bt_test_stop`. If test mode is active, the function will not proceed and will send a response indicating the invalid operation. This function sets the system to override mode and disables the Bluetooth inhibitor, preparing the system for testing.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_test_start`](<bt_compliance_tests.c.md#bt_test_start>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_test\_stop<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_test_stop}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/bt_compliance_tests.h#L23>)

Stops the Bluetooth test mode.
- **Description**: Use this function to stop the Bluetooth test mode that was previously started. It must be called only after the test mode has been enabled using the appropriate start function. If the test mode is not active, the function will not perform any operations and will notify the user of the invalid operation. This function also restores the normal Bluetooth stack operation.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_test_stop`](<bt_compliance_tests.c.md#bt_test_stop>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_test\_bt\_sig\_rf\_test\_mode<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_test_bt_sig_rf_test_mode}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/bt_compliance_tests.h#L25>)

Checks if the Bluetooth SIG RF test mode is active.
- **Description**: Use this function to verify if the Bluetooth SIG RF test mode is currently active. This function is useful in scenarios where you need to confirm the test mode status before performing operations that depend on this mode. It returns a boolean value indicating the status of the test mode.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the Bluetooth SIG RF test mode is active, otherwise returns `false`.
- **See Also**: [`bt_test_bt_sig_rf_test_mode`](<bt_compliance_tests.c.md#bt_test_bt_sig_rf_test_mode>)  (Implementation)


---
### bt\_test\_enter\_hci\_passthrough<!-- {{#callable_declaration:bt_test_enter_hci_passthrough}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/bluetooth/bt_compliance_tests.h#L26>)

Enables HCI passthrough mode for Bluetooth testing.
- **Description**: Use this function to enable HCI passthrough mode, which redirects all communications to the Bluetooth module. This mode is typically used for testing purposes and should be activated when you need to directly interact with the Bluetooth hardware through HCI commands. Ensure that the system is in a state where entering passthrough mode will not disrupt ongoing operations.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`bt_test_enter_hci_passthrough`](<bt_compliance_tests.c.md#bt_test_enter_hci_passthrough>)  (Implementation)
